excluded_defs.name: EmailValidator
excluded_defs.reason: read as a reference pattern for string property validators; not changed

exploration_log.aha_moment: EmailValidator and AspNetCoreCompatibleEmailValidator show the exact pattern - a PropertyValidator<T,string> with a Name, IsValid, and GetDefaultMessageTemplate - and the extension method just calls SetValidator. Uri.TryCreate is the standard .NET URL parsing API.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could also have stored the AllowedSchemes as a HashSet for O(1) lookup, but for the small sets typically used (e.g., ['https']), an array with LINQ Contains is fine.

key_decisions.decision: Use Uri.TryCreate() for validation as specified in the task
key_decisions.reasoning: Standard .NET URI parsing, handles edge cases correctly without regex complexity

queries.justification: Rule compliance: uses only domain concepts (URL validation, URI scheme, host requirement) without any code terms. Pre-implementation: a developer adding a URL validator would search this.
queries.query_text: Add a built-in URL validator that checks if a string is a valid URI with optional allowed schemes and host requirements

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 'is not a valid URL' appears literally in the new English message. Pre-implementation: a developer might search for similar error message patterns.
Rule compliance: lists 4 exact symbol names from the new code. Pre-implementation: a developer knows these from the task description.
Rule compliance: names UrlValidator with implementors/callers relationship. Pre-implementation: an agent would look at what PropertyValidator implementors look like.
queries.query_text: Implementors of PropertyValidator<T,string> that validate URI format, and callers of IsUrl extension method

queries.justification: Rule compliance: provides 2 explicit file paths. Pre-implementation: task names both files.
Rule compliance: mixes domain concept (URL validation, scheme constraints) with 3 exact symbol names. Pre-implementation: natural query from the task description.
queries.query_text: Add UrlValidator using Uri.TryCreate with AllowedSchemes constraint, wired via IsUrl extension method

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 3 symbol names plus 2 file paths. Pre-implementation: combines known files with expected symbols.
Rule compliance: contains symbol names and file paths. Pre-implementation: natural full query.
queries.query_text: Add UrlValidator.cs using Uri.TryCreate with AllowedSchemes and RequireHost, IsUrl() extension in DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs, and UrlValidator error message in EnglishLanguage.cs

solve_notes: Created `UrlValidator<T>` in `Validators/UrlValidator.cs` using `Uri.TryCreate()` with `AllowedSchemes` (list-based scheme whitelist) and `RequireHost` parameters. Added `IsUrl()` extension in `DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs` and registered the default error message in `EnglishLanguage.cs`.
task_text:
There is no built-in URL validator. Add a `UrlValidator` in
`Validators/UrlValidator.cs` that validates string properties as
well-formed URIs using `Uri.TryCreate()`, with optional `AllowedSchemes`
(e.g., `["https"]`) and `RequireHost` parameters. Wire it with an
`IsUrl()` extension method in `DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs` and
register a default error message in `LanguageManager`.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: AspNetCoreCompatibleEmailValidator
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: reference for the PropertyValidator<T,string> pattern - confirms class structure, Name property, IsValid signature, GetDefaultMessageTemplate

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers all edited/created files. An AI agent would also inspect the existing email validator to confirm the exact structure before creating a new string property validator, preventing an incorrect class hierarchy or method signature.
